item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate what you were going for, and I feel like this concept could have been executed really well, but the end product felt like it was retreading ideas other skips have done better. I also only laughed once, the rest of the humor moments really felt forced or missed the mark for me ('missed the mark' is a phrase, right? It feels weird for some reason.)

Overall decent but still a -1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm in agreement with this. The staff antics got old after a few seconds, and throughout the entire read I was just thinking "4498 did this better".

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but gonna be honest, this doesn't go far enough. Site19 sprouting chicken legs and making a fucking run for it is a fantastic idea, and having a bunch of senior staff inside of it who absolutely loose it when it happens is phenomenal but the execution here is short of what it could have been. It doesn't have the punch or panache of Kaktus' rendition of 4498, or the bombastic-ness of something like from Dusk till Dawn.

The characters feel rote, and mostly one note. I think the worst offender here is probably Clef, who has a grand total of 4-5 lines, and none of his dialogue or actions really feel Clefish. Bright is alright. Crow is adequate. But everyone else feels criminally underused, and their interactions less zany/insane than they really should be in this kind of lolfoundation situation. In other words, the piece goes too far while simultaneously doesn't go far enough into the mind-boggling insanity to fit into the absurdity. Instead it feels like an underdeveloped -J and because of that I have to down-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know LolFondation is frowned upon, but I'm honestly glad we can take a moment to be silly from time to time. It's, what, 5 out of 1000 skips? Not exactly the end of the site. And hey, Kondraki's back. That's… A bomb waiting to go off, be careful with that

All around hilarious. A few writing errors you should correct, though. Namely "afer" in the afterword of addendum 3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a Terrible Person who always the senior staff antics hilarious1 (and precisely because of the tonal whiplash between it and the geniunely creepy stuff), I found this both a complete surprise (as a break from my very, very slow reading through everything down the lists, I just decided to look what the top of the "top rated recent" column was) and an absolute delight.

Very much +1!

Footnotes
1. I mean, I find Teen Titans Go funny, so, I know I'm bad…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ridiculous. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have much to add other then that this is the funniest non-J class SCP I've probably ever read. It's just funny to imagine everything just devolving into chaos as the building your inside grows legs and starts running around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a new reader I found this fun, interesting and weird enough to deserve both an SCP classified file and a +1. I can see how it's not for everyone but I doubt anything on this site is 'for' everyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm on the anti-lolfoundation boat, even when it's "toned down". I don't mind articles with the senior avatars, but that's when they act like real people would.

But I enjoyed this, a lot. And why would I read SCP if not to enjoy myself? Whether it's horror, psychological, or comedy, entertainment is entertainment and the website has catered different genres for a while now. Some SCPs have a serious tone and are there for the overall experience rather than "fun" fun, but I honestly don't mind reading some silly stuff once in a while, and it's not like one article is gonna bring down the overall tone of the site that has almost 5000 skips.

+1

Also making the chicken legs building-sized rather than chicken-sized is a coward's move.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this, even though I feel it's too funny to be on the main SCP list.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really care about the rest of this. But

I really don't think articles like this add anything to the wiki

is patently absurd to the point of insulting.

This one doesn't work for me. It feels like it's trying very hard to surpass 4498, but ends up lacking its own distinct substance. I smirked at meta jokes, but I also feel like admitting that addenda is boring doesn't really resolve the problem of it being, well, boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly? Not even the strangest, dumbest, most ridiculous thing I've read on this site… Got close to making me laugh out loud in class, so that's a plus.
Part of Site-19 growing chicke legs and making a run for it is probably not that weird by Bright-Clef-Kaktus standards, so… I'll allow it even if it could fit in better as a -J or lolFoundation (But really? I don't think anybody cares)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how this is at all alienating to new readers? You're free to have your own opinion on whether or not it's funny, but I fail to see how this article is some great blunder that spells doom and gloom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
look maybe I just don't have a sense of humor anymore but the lolfoundation stuff is so played out to me. There was not a single line of this past the opening description that I didn't find tedious.

I'm sorry, but this is a firm -1. I really don't think articles like this add anything to the wiki. They're alienating for newcomers and if you've read this place since it was new like I have you just feel like you've seen it a million times before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: u rite u rite

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: second log says "T-MINUTES" instead of "T-MINUS"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, these Eberstrom’s Proposal rewrites are getting intense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No kidding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like this. I think it's too lolFoundation - style.

item.title: When you walkin
item.content:encoded:
This really feels like a -J, but it also feels like a mainlister.
+1 for funny.

while I feel that having overwatch command also play into the silliness was a bit too much (it makes sense with even the site-19 command seeing as how the whole point of the article is "hey look how crazy these people are". they could've made for a good straight-man kinda role), it was still a very enjoyable read and was still absurdly funny.

also the "boring audio logs" bit really got me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This felt so much like a J to me…

But in the end, it isn't. Do I care? No. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should be a -J.

But yeah, I like it, +1, the whole shebang.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just barely weird enough for an upvote. I think the egg-laying pushed it over for me. I wasn't a huge fan of the staff antics humor though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i agree with ARD here. it's funny at points but it feels *too* self aware and insane for its own good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The other comments explain in better detail why this is so funny, so I won't try. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I preferred the senior staff meltdown in SCP-4498. This one tries its best to be as goofy and funny but isn’t willing to fully commit to insanity, so all the character dynamics feel trite instead of satisfying.

item.title: site 19 be walkin
item.content:encoded:
I liked this greatly. The humor lands most of the time and it works very well.
And of course, site 19 be walkin

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What Meats said is basically the reason I liked this in crit. Duly updooted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn that was a lot of author avatars.

Got a few solid chuckles from me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really gratuitous but you went over the top enough to resurrect the energy and bring it together. The lampshading is dry and witty and, despite everything going on here, the action is compact and efficient to the point that *nothing* is overstayed.

This is good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SITE-19 BE WALKIN


this is a funny little comedy piece i wanted to push out as fast as possible. lolfoundation ain't for everyone and a lot of pieces don't do it for me, but i hope you enjoy this one.

Who To Blame

thanks to my beta readers: aismallard, nicolini, kirby, rattles, jackal, rockteethmotheyes, coryn02, & pokemonprime.

Who To REALLY Blame

ratking/pastarasta1, who made the joke that inspired this glorious trainwreck.

IMAGE CC.BY.SA.2.0
edited by me.

If you want to go the Rounderpage, for some ungodly reason.
